target: HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 1 (major): Eclipse/Chapman cluster has gotten WORSE since c1. c2 finds 39 separate re-derivations of the Chapman 1933 / 5–25 nT range across five sections (part4 21, part5 6, part6 3, part8 2, part8b 7). The part8b §8b.3 addition is the largest single contributor — its four-layer adoption stack substantially re-derives the same arithmetic §4.2.4 already presents. c2 designates §4.2 as the canonical hub (confirmed correct) and asks for: (a) §8b.3 reduced to fair-auditor-list-specific framing + cross-reference §4.2.4 for Chapman comparison; (b) §6.7 / §6.14 prospective-bucket and κ-vise sections to retain only their new argumentative content (cross-event coupling, prospective-vs-retrodictive sorting) and reference §4.2 for Chapman.
short_recommendation: Apply two coordinated cuts: (P0) reduce §8b.3 from 12,855 chars (~2,100 words) to ~6,300 chars (~1,050 words) by collapsing the three "L3a (PRED-095)" paragraphs into a single one-paragraph summary that cross-references §4.2.4 (E-PRED arithmetic), §4.2.5 (no-dome-geometry structural problem), and §4.2.3 (hedging / 0 nT strawman); retain the four-layer adoption framing, the per-layer adoption labels (L1/L2/L3a/L3b/L4), the dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis (genuinely novel here), and the (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) checklist scaffold. (P1) Add one sentence in §6.14 above the dual-formula audit that says "the magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument lives at §4.2.5 — this section adds only the cross-event vise (Mohe→Wang κ→1999/2009 SG null) and the internal-formula heterogeneity (BOU vs W004 vs monitor.py KAPPA²)." Estimated recoverable words: ~1,050 in §8b.3 + ~150 in §6.14 = ~1,200 (c2 estimated ~3,000; the lower estimate is honest — the canonical-hub commitment is mostly enforced by removing wholesale paragraphs, not by trimming every sentence).
kernel_of_truth: §8b.3 is doing genuinely valuable work that no other section does: it adjudicates the dome author's own fair-auditor checklist on its own terms, point-by-point, with (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) "what he asks / charitable read / what changes under adoption / adopt-modify-reject" structure. The four-layer L1/L2/L3a/L3b/L4 framing is itself the steel-man response — it gives the dome author a concrete answer to "audit the eclipse stack in layers" while preserving our structural critiques. The dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis (interpretation A baseline-null vs interpretation B Chapman-mechanism-overlap) is novel argumentation that exists nowhere else in the document. And the §6.14 κ-vise / cross-event coupling argument is also genuinely unique — Mohe→κ→1999/2009 SG-null is a cross-event constraint that §4.2 does not address. So the c2 critique is NOT "this content shouldn't exist" but rather "the arithmetic exposition supporting these unique points is duplicating §4.2.4 verbatim and should be replaced with a cross-reference." That's a precise diagnosis and the fix is correspondingly precise.
why_it_doesnt_save_the_argument: The Chapman 5–25 nT range and the (baseline × coverage × geometry) arithmetic exist in §4.2.4/§4.2.5 in their canonical form and have been there since the V6 restructure. Every re-derivation in §8b.3, §6.7, and §6.14 is a re-derivation, not an extension. Each one was written at a different time, in a different rhetorical frame, by a different agent run — and the cumulative effect is the c2 finding: a defender skimming our document sees "one good point dressed up as six" (c2 Hole 4 framing, applied here). The fact that each individual paragraph is well-written does not save the document from looking padded. Removing the duplicate exposition does not weaken any argument — it strengthens them by letting the canonical hub carry the weight and freeing the satellite sections to do their unique work.

Not bundled here because it is a different rhetorical pattern (strawman attribution) than the magnitude-band re-derivation pattern (P0/P1).

All other content in §8b.3 — the four-layer adoption framing, L1/L2/L3b/L4 paragraphs, the (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) checklist, the (d) "Unchanged" summary, the ADOPTED label — stays as-is.

proposed_integration_patches.replacement_html_for_l3a_block: L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — codified Pass band, derivation, and non-discrimination (consolidated): Genuinely prospective (dome registration 2026-03-12 via Git + OpenTimestamps; our tracker entry 2026-04-04; window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending). Under adoption we publish an explicit codified threshold — our Pass band — defined as “observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone.” This band is our steel-man characterization of the dome’s W004-cluster prediction; the dome itself publishes per-station predictions across a dual-baseline structure (BOU 2017 per-station ∼−10 nT, W004 empirical per-station ∼−20 nT). PRED-095 carries a derivation path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF), so the complaint is not “the dome has no formula” — it is that every input traces to globe infrastructure per the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint and the dome author’s own OPEN-001 admission that “dome-native coordinates without WGS84” remains IN PROGRESS. The four E-PRED sub-tests, the structural “no dome geometry enters the formula” argument, and the dual-baseline hedging that frames any quiet-day result as confirmation are presented in canonical form at §4.2.4 (the four E-PRED tests), §4.2.5 (structural problems), and §4.2.3 (the hedging structure) respectively; the internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity is audited at §6.14 (the κ coupling cluster). Two issues survive consolidation and are unique to this fair-auditor-list framing: the published W004 cluster is a selection from coexisting dome formulas rather than a forced derivation, and our codified Fail-clause — the phrase “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” that we appended to our Pass band — admits two natural readings, and the dome has not published any falsification criterion specific enough to disambiguate (PRED-095 as registered in data/predictions.json contains no Chapman-language Fail criterion at all, so any Chapman-language fair-audit Fail-clause inherits the ambiguity below). Read as a baseline-Sq null test (interpretation A — “the observed signal is indistinguishable from background Sq variation, without any eclipse-driven enhancement”), the Fail-clause is a fair null check that rules out dome and standard Chapman equally, because both models require an eclipse-specific enhancement; there is no tension with Pass, but this reading also concedes that a Pass in our codified band carries no dome-specific forecast, since Chapman’s mechanism is the Sq-dynamo and already predicts an eclipse-driven enhancement in the same magnitude range. Read as targeting the eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo (interpretation B — “the observed signal can be explained by the Chapman mechanism’s response to the eclipse alone, without dome input”), Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal. The dome has not committed to either disambiguation, and under either reading the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe — reading A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible.

deferred_items: Original §8b.3 anchor id verification. At the time of EXP authoring the part8b html block ID for §8b.3 appears as <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> in the live html string in data/sections.json. If a prior decider patch has renamed this anchor (e.g., to p8b-3-eclipse-stack), the integration must use the live id. Use grep at integration time: `grep -oP '<details id="p8b-3[^"]*"' data/sections.json`.

Hole 8 (0 nT strawman) is a separate sweep across §4.2.3, §4.2/5/6/8/8b that warrants its own EXP. ISS-2326-ish naming would make the consolidation parallel to ISS-2324 (this EXP) and ISS-2325 / ISS-2327. We do NOT bundle it here because the 0 nT strawman pattern is a different rhetorical pattern (mis-attribution to standard physics) from the magnitude-band re-derivation pattern (mathematical duplication), and the appropriate canonical hub is different (§4.2.3 for hedging vs §4.2.4/§4.2.5 for arithmetic). Bundling would cause this EXP to address two structurally distinct problems and would risk under-treating both.

A sweep across §5.0 Kill-Shot Test 5 and §8.11 for shorter Chapman re-derivations (5–25 nT references that c2 counted in the 39-total). These are smaller per-instance — typically a single sentence or two — and the consolidation pattern (find the sentence, replace with "see §4.2.4 / §4.2.5") is mechanical. A baby-batch verification-class EXP is the right vehicle once the §8b.3 anchor change above lands. Not bundled here because the anchor-id update from this EXP must land first; otherwise the cross-references in the sweep point at stale ids.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are removing 1,050+ words of audited, citation-rich content. The L3a paragraphs include specific Pass-band numerics, dome OPEN-001 admissions, and the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint linkage — these are the strongest empirical bindings in §8b.3. Cutting them weakens the section.
anticipated_objections.response: The consolidation preserves every empirical binding in the one consolidated paragraph: the codified Pass band (−17 to −21 nT, >80% coverage, ≥3 sites), the dome's dual-baseline structure (BOU per-station ~−10 nT, W004 per-station ~−20 nT), the formula path (ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF), the WIN-025 globe-fingerprint linkage, and the OPEN-001 dome-native-coordinates-IN-PROGRESS admission. What is removed is the second-pass exposition of these same facts in the non-discrimination and Fail-clause analysis paragraphs — and that exposition is replaced with cross-references to §4.2.4/§4.2.5/§4.2.3 where the same facts are presented in their canonical form. A reader who wants the full arithmetic clicks the cross-reference; a reader who wants the fair-auditor-list adjudication stays in §8b.3 and reads the consolidated paragraph plus the dual-Sq-reading novel argumentation.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis (interpretation A baseline-null vs interpretation B Chapman-mechanism-overlap) is preserved verbatim in the consolidation — but it sits at the end of one giant paragraph instead of having its own paragraph break. That hurts readability.
anticipated_objections.response:
Fair point. The consolidated paragraph is ~620 words and the dual-Sq analysis is ~280 words of that. Breaking it into its own paragraph (with a "

Two issues survive consolidation..." or similar transition) is cosmetic and the integrator (decider) is free to choose the break. The replacement_html_for_l3a_block in P0 above presents it as one paragraph for clarity of the replacement boundaries; the decider can split into two paragraphs at integration time without changing the argument.

anticipated_objections.objection: You assume §4.2.4, §4.2.5, §4.2.3 will not themselves be edited or renumbered. If they move, the cross-references break.
anticipated_objections.response: The §4.2.x subsections have been stable since the V6 restructure (2026-04-07). HOL-REDUNDANCY c1 already named §4.2 as the canonical eclipse hub; c2 confirms §4.2 has stayed the hub. HOL-HIERARCHY c2 (EXP-450) restructured §2.x not §4.x. The risk is low. If a future restructure does renumber §4.2.x, the cross-references in §8b.3 are part of a broader cross-reference graph that an integrity-agent pass can update mechanically — the consolidation does not introduce new fragility, it leverages existing fragility.

anticipated_objections.objection: You estimated ~1,050 recoverable words; c2 estimated ~3,000. The gap implies either you missed cuts or c2 was over-optimistic.
anticipated_objections.response: Honest accounting: c2 included the §6.7 prospective-test re-derivation and the §6.11 prospective-bucket and §4.2 itself (where some Chapman repetition exists internally to §4.2.x). I deliberately scoped this EXP narrower: §8b.3 ~1,050 + §6.14 pointer ~150 = ~1,200. The remaining ~1,800 words c2 had in mind live in: (a) §6.7's "previous eclipse magnetic claims failed" history exposition (~300 words, low-priority because §6.7 is already the genuine-test-or-pre-fitted analysis, not pure Chapman re-derivation; cutting would weaken the prediction-history-failure argument); (b) §5.0 Kill-Shot Test 5 (~400 words, deferred to the §5 sweep mentioned in deferred_items[2]); (c) intra-§4.2 internal repetition between §4.2.4 and §4.2.5 (~400 words, requires careful editing of the canonical hub itself — high blast radius if done wrong, low priority because the hub IS allowed to be longer than satellite sections); (d) §8.11 and §6.11/§6.12 minor re-derivations (~700 words combined, deferred to baby-batch sweep). The c2 estimate is reachable but not in one EXP — three coordinated EXPs is the right granularity, and this is the first and largest.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why insert the §6.14 pointer ABOVE the "What κ is" h3 instead of inside it as a parenthetical? An h3-prefix paragraph reads like throat-clearing.
anticipated_objections.response: The pointer is scaffolding — it tells the reader "you are not about to read what you read in §4.2.5; you are about to read the κ-vise and formula-heterogeneity argument that §4.2.5 does not cover." Putting it above the h3 makes the scaffolding visible without polluting the κ-derivation prose. A parenthetical inside the h3 body would read as a defensive caveat ("oh by the way we already covered the non-discrimination argument elsewhere") rather than as a navigation aid. The decider may relocate the sentence if integration preference differs, but the function — a one-sentence "the magnitude-band argument lives at §4.2.5, this section assumes it" — is the load-bearing edit.

recommended_decider_action.summary: Apply P0 then P1 in a single commit. Both are clean replacements: P0 replaces three paragraphs with one, P1 inserts one paragraph above an h3. No data values change. Tests should pass without modification (no numeric claims added or removed). Route to curmudgeon as review_class=deep-attack (this EXP introduces a consolidated paragraph that the curmudgeon has not seen, even though every individual claim within it pre-exists).
recommended_decider_action.test_expectation: tests/run.sh should pass cleanly. No numbers are added, removed, or changed. The consolidation moves text and replaces it with cross-references; no quantitative claims are altered.
recommended_decider_action.queue_class_recommendation: deep-attack (singleton). Even though the change is consolidation not new argumentation, the curmudgeon needs to verify (a) no novel claim was smuggled in, (b) the cross-references actually carry the load the consolidation assigns them, (c) the §6.14 pointer reads as scaffolding not as a thesis-walk-back.

summary_for_decider: EXP-462 addresses HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 1 (ISS-2324) with two coordinated patches that consolidate the largest single contributor to the eclipse/Chapman 39-occurrence redundancy cluster. P0 collapses §8b.3's three "L3a (PRED-095)" paragraphs (~1,800 words combined) into a single ~620-word paragraph that preserves every empirical binding (codified Pass band, dual-baseline structure, ΔZ formula path, WIN-025 globe-fingerprint linkage, OPEN-001 dome-native-coords admission, dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis) and cross-references §4.2.4/§4.2.5/§4.2.3/§6.14 for the canonical arithmetic, structural-problem, hedging, and cross-event analyses. The four-layer adoption framing, L1/L2/L3b/L4 paragraphs, (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) checklist, and ADOPTED label all stay intact — §8b.3's unique fair-auditor-list-adjudication contribution is preserved. P1 inserts a one-sentence canonical-hub pointer at the top of §6.14's ps-detail body that defers the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument to §4.2.5, leaving §6.14 free to be the cross-event-κ-vise and internal-formula-heterogeneity section it already is. Total recoverable words: ~1,200 (vs c2's ~3,000 estimate — see anticipated_objections[3] for honest accounting of the gap). review_class=deep-attack because the consolidated paragraph is new even though every individual claim pre-exists; curmudgeon needs to verify nothing novel was smuggled and that the cross-references carry the load. Deferred to follow-up EXPs: a §5/§6.7/§8.11 sweep for shorter re-derivations (verification-class, batchable), and Hole 8 (0 nT strawman, ISS-2326-equivalent — different rhetorical pattern, separate canonical hub at §4.2.3). Tests should pass without modification. Estimated curmudgeon review surface: 1 EXP, ~6 KB diff to read.
